slides.narration: Tour of ToonStyles — a Next.js app that turns a photo of your kid into a personalized backpack design. By the end you'll know the full pipeline, from upload to checkout.

data.title: Inside ToonStyles
data.subtitle: A Next.js shop that AI-generates personalized kids' backpacks

slides.narration: The whole product in three moves. Upload a photo, let the AI drop the kid onto a dozen designs, then check out with Stripe.

data.heading: What ToonStyles does, in three moves
items: Parent uploads a photo and picks hobbies and cartoons the kid likes.
Backend detects the face, then composites it onto many design templates.
Generated backpacks land in a cart — Stripe handles checkout.

slides.narration: The layout. Everything lives under src. The wizard drives the user through five steps, and the generate API route is where the real work happens.
Three layers. A React wizard in the browser, a Next.js API route in the middle, and a stack of external services doing the heavy lifting.

Here's one generation, end to end. The API fires face detection and the design fetch in parallel, sends each cropped face to Replicate, composites the result with Sharp, and uploads to Supabase storage.
The trick that keeps it snappy. Face detection takes about six seconds, fetching designs takes about two. Running them sequentially costs eight — running them together costs six.

data.heading: Parallel streaming inside /api/generate
data.caption: route.ts — two slow tasks, one Promise.all

slides.narration: A nice optimization on top. If the frontend already ran the GLM detection once, it sends the cached crop back on the next request and the API skips the whole face step.

data.title: Cached crop skip
data.body: If the wizard already holds the GLM result from a prior request, it re-sends it as cachedCropData. The API short-circuits face detection entirely.

slides.narration: Three things that will trip up a newcomer. Designs are silently filtered by gender and age. Authentication uses anonymous Supabase sessions. And admin login is a single hardcoded password.

data.title: Gotchas worth knowing
pitfalls: Designs with gender or age_range constraints are silently skipped if GLM's guess doesn't match.
Most users are anonymous Supabase sessions — past designs only persist in localStorage.
Admin login is a single env-var password check, not real auth.

slides.narration: The whole thing in one breath. A wizard collects inputs, a single API route orchestrates four external services in parallel, Sharp does the final composite, and Stripe closes the loop.

data.heading: Takeaways
points: One big API route — /api/generate — runs the whole AI pipeline.
Promise.all plus a cached crop keeps generation under seven seconds.
Sharp composites the Replicate poster onto a backpack mockup server-side.
Stripe checkout + webhook persists the order into Supabase.
